summary: Coverage crisis: 1 test file covers 1 pure utility module (33 API routes + 770-line iron-law hot file totally untested), no `npm test` script, CI runs only lint/typecheck/build. Every documented post-disappearance regression is unwatched.

framework_recommendation.rationale: Vitest is the only viable choice: native ESM + TS, first-class Next 16 / React 19 support, jest-compatible API, JSdom for client hooks, and parallel test isolation. Existing node:test run command (`node --test --import tsx`) is too anemic to mock Supabase + DOM. Add Playwright in a follow-on for the iron-law board flow (drag move, refresh-without-vanish).

findings.title: No test locks the load() fallback-to-PLACEHOLDER bug — THE iron-law regression
findings.regression_risk: Reintroducing `result.data.length > 0` guard would resurrect the silent posts-disappear-on-refresh bug. AGENTS.md §1b lists this as THE single most important rule. Currently zero automated detection.
findings.fix: Add `src/lib/__tests__/pipeline-context.load.test.tsx` covering: empty array → empty UI, error → localStorage fallback, real rows → mapped cards, FULL-select error → BASIC-select retry.

findings.title: No test asserts createCard always sends workspace_id
findings.regression_risk: AGENTS.md §1c: omitting workspace_id silently fails the INSERT (NOT NULL constraint, no DEFAULT). The card vanishes on next refresh. Pattern can be rewritten to a conditional in a future refactor and silently break.
findings.fix: Add `pipeline-context.createCard.test.tsx`. Snapshot the insertRow argument and assert workspace_id key presence under both null and populated ref.

findings.title: No test for isValidUuid guard on .eq('id', tempId) — covers 5 call sites
findings.branch_or_function: moveCard / submitReapproval / submitKickback / updateCard / deleteCard — `if (useSupabase && isValidUuid(cardId))`
findings.regression_risk: Removing any one of these 5 guards lets a temp-id timestamp string hit PostgREST, returns 400, triggers rollback, card snaps back. Five separate regressions can shed each guard independently — no test currently catches the loss.
findings.fix: Single parametric test that loops over every mutator and confirms no Supabase call fires when the id is a tempId string.

findings.title: No test confirms /api/workspace/provision is called before posts SELECT
findings.branch_or_function: load() — provision fetch then setWorkspaceId then supabase.select
findings.regression_risk: AGENTS.md §4: if provision never fires, the user isn't in workspace_members, RLS returns 0 rows, page looks empty. Order-of-operations bug — easy to break by moving the fetch.
findings.fix: Order-of-effects test in pipeline-context.test.tsx using a fetch + supabase call recorder.

findings.title: No test for the 0015 delete-protection trigger — UI thinks delete worked when DB blocked it
findings.regression_risk: If the regex breaks or the error message changes, the card stays removed in UI while the row survives in DB → divergence → user reloads, post reappears, looks 'undeleted'. AGENTS.md iron-law spirit.
findings.fix: Add `pipeline-context.deleteCard.test.tsx` covering: success, protected-by-trigger, generic-error, and non-uuid-skip cases.

findings.title: No integration test for migration 0015 triggers on real Postgres
findings.regression_risk: AGENTS.md §1a documents these triggers must NOT be dropped or disabled. A future migration could `drop trigger` accidentally and nothing would warn. Memory[reference_unwired_infrastructure]: code shipping without callers is theatrical — same applies to triggers shipping without tests.
findings.fix: Add `supabase/__tests__/0015_post_safety.integration.test.ts` using a local Supabase (`supabase start`) and the supabase CLI. Run as a separate CI job: `npm run test:db`.

findings.title: CI has no `test` step — pipeline can ship without running the one test that exists
findings.regression_risk: Memory[reference_unwired_infrastructure] precedent: aspect-resolver.test.ts ships with no caller. There is no `npm test` script in package.json AND no CI step would call it if it existed. New tests added per this report would also be theatrical until both are wired.
findings.test_outline: n/a — infra fix.
findings.fix: 1) Add `"test": "vitest run"` and `"test:watch": "vitest"` to package.json scripts. 2) Add a `Test` step to ci.yml after Typecheck. 3) Add `"preflight": "npm ci && npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm test && npm run build"`. 4) Wire coverage upload with `vitest run --coverage` and a coverage threshold floor (start at 0%, ratchet weekly).

findings.title: No test for /api/workspace/provision self-healing flow
findings.branch_or_function: GET handler — token verify, existing membership shortcut, team_members lookup, insert
findings.regression_risk: This route is the chicken-and-egg breaker for RLS. If GET handler returns 500 or stops calling listUsers correctly, every new user sees empty board on first login. The fence is on the client side too — both sides need to be locked.
findings.fix: Add `src/app/api/workspace/provision/__tests__/route.test.ts` with a mocked admin client.

findings.title: No test for /api/publish-jobs role gate + stage gate
findings.branch_or_function: POST — WRITE_ROLES membership check + stage==='approved_scheduled' guard
findings.regression_risk: If WRITE_ROLES set drifts (e.g. dropping 'specialist') or the stage check is relaxed, viewers could trigger publish jobs or non-approved posts could go live. This is the n8n trigger surface — silent breakage = real money/brand damage.
findings.fix: Route test with mocked Supabase admin + caller clients.

findings.title: No test for /api/team/invite — auth-elevation surface
findings.regression_risk: This route mints auth users. A regression that drops the requireBearerTeamRole check, accepts client-supplied requestedBy, or skips email validation could let anyone provision admins. Audit-log call is best-effort (try/catch) so failure is silent.
findings.fix: Route test with mocked admin client + listUsers paginator + nodemailer.

findings.title: audit.ts wrapper: no test for the isValidUuid → null mapping in record_audit_event
findings.regression_risk: If isValidUuid is broken or removed here, temp-id strings hit a UUID column and the RPC errors silently (logAudit doesn't surface errors). All audit trails for that action go missing. Memory[reference_unwired_infrastructure]: audit_log_v2 view is the source of truth; broken writes here mean blank actor history.
findings.fix: Add `src/lib/__tests__/audit.test.ts` mocking supabase.rpc and supabase.from.

findings.title: No test that audit writes use record_audit_event RPC, never post_audit_logs INSERT
findings.regression_risk: AGENTS.md §3: client INSERT to post_audit_logs is silently RLS-blocked. If a future PR adds `supabase.from('post_audit_logs').insert(...)` anywhere on the client, audit goes dark with no error.
findings.test_outline: Static lint rule + unit test: grep src/ (excluding src/app/api/**, src/lib/database.types.ts, and asset-review-drawer.tsx which reads only) for `from("post_audit_logs")` and fail if any INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE call appears.
findings.fix: Add a vitest test using `fs.readdirSync` + regex, OR an ESLint custom rule `no-client-audit-table` (cheaper). Health-check route reads are allowed.

findings.title: No test for requireRole / requireBearerTeamRole helpers
findings.branch_or_function: requireRole + requireBearerTeamRole — workspace_members status='active' + role allowlist
findings.regression_risk: Every API route that uses these inherits whatever they return. A bug here is a cross-cutting auth hole. Memory[reference_oauth_state_csrf_pattern] precedent — auth code needs constant-time + edge-case coverage.
findings.fix: Add `src/lib/auth/__tests__/require.test.ts`. Mock createClient and createServerSupabaseClient.

findings.title: No test for the optimistic-rollback path in moveCard
findings.branch_or_function: moveCard catch block — rollback() + addToast + DB rollback via second update
findings.regression_risk: On Supabase error, UI must roll back. If `stageUpdated` flag logic regresses, the UI stays in the new stage while the DB stays in the old stage → divergence on refresh. AGENTS.md iron-law spirit.
findings.fix: Add cases to `pipeline-context.moveCard.test.tsx`.

findings.title: No test for realtime echo dedup via markMutation()
findings.branch_or_function: recentMutations set + markMutation + INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE realtime handlers
findings.regression_risk: Two simultaneous creators previously collided on literal 'create' key (fix at line 699). Realtime regressions show up as duplicate cards, ghost cards, or 'undeleted' cards. Hard to manually QA, easy to lock in test.
findings.fix: Add `pipeline-context.realtime.test.tsx`. Use vi.useFakeTimers for the 2s window.

findings.title: No test for toScheduledAt timezone math — silent data corruption surface
findings.regression_risk: Scheduled-post-publishes-at-wrong-time is one of the highest-impact silent bugs in a social calendar product. The TZ-correction math is non-trivial; a one-off DST/JS Date refactor could shift everything by an hour without anyone noticing.
findings.fix: Pure-function test in `pipeline-context.scheduling.test.ts` — fastest test in the suite.

findings.title: supabase-js 2.100+ Database<> generic resolves to never — types regeneration is not enforced
findings.branch_or_function: scripts.db:types — manual codegen, no CI check
findings.regression_risk: Memory[reference_supabase_js_v2.100_typing] documents the never-resolution failure mode. Currently `src/lib/database.types.ts` does NOT exist (db:types has never been run locally). `db:types:check` exists but is not in CI. The Database generic is effectively dead, all .from() calls run untyped — typecheck passes without catching column drift like migrations 0013/0019 cause.
findings.test_outline: n/a — infra fix.
findings.fix: 1) Run `npm run db:types` once to commit `src/lib/database.types.ts`. 2) Add `scripts.db:types:check` to the CI workflow after typecheck (fails if committed types diverge from DB schema). 3) Adopt the row-cast-at-page-boundary pattern from [[reference_supabase_js_v2.100_typing]] for the Database generic on supabaseClient.ts.

findings.title: No tests for the 4 Studio API routes (rows, generate-batch, generate-row, cancel-job)
findings.regression_risk: Studio path has cost guards ($3/row cap, daily budget) and 14-row batch ceiling. If `.slice(0, 14)` drops or the isValidUuid filter regresses, agents could burn through OpenAI budget without enforcement. Memory[project_ten80ten_smm_ai_content_plan].
findings.fix: Suite under `src/app/api/ai/studio/__tests__/`. Mock openai + supabase.

findings.title: No E2E playwright test for the iron-law board flow
findings.regression_risk: Component-level vitest tests can lock individual functions but cannot catch the integration regression where, say, provision returns 500 + load() falls back + cards look fine until refresh. Only E2E catches the user-visible incident.
findings.fix: Add Playwright in a follow-on. `npm i -D @playwright/test`, point at a seeded Supabase branch, run nightly + on `main` push.

findings.title: No test that migrations are idempotent (re-runs don't error)
findings.branch_or_function: drop trigger if exists + create trigger — pattern across all migrations 0015-0026
findings.regression_risk: A future migration could omit `drop ... if exists`, breaking `supabase db reset` for any dev who already applied that version. Also blocks CI seeding a clean Postgres.
findings.fix: Single test using node-postgres + the same temp DB harness as TEST-006.

findings.title: Static-grep test: forbidden patterns in pipeline-context.tsx + callers
findings.regression_risk: Belt-and-suspenders over the unit tests: even if a unit test gets deleted, a grep test catches the literal anti-pattern. Cheap insurance.
findings.fix: Add `src/lib/__tests__/iron-law-static.test.ts` with 5 grep tests aligned to AGENTS.md §1b, §1c, §3, §5, §8 checklist.

findings.title: No test for studio-smoke.mjs harness
findings.regression_risk: Smoke script is run manually pre-launch; if broken it gives false-green. Low priority because it's a dev tool, not user-facing.
findings.test_outline: test('studio-smoke exits 0 against staging', async () => {...});
findings.fix: Wire as a separate `test:smoke` script invoked only on demand.

findings.title: Notification routes have no contract tests
findings.branch_or_function: POST handlers — body shape, audit-log call, email render
findings.regression_risk: If a notification handler crashes on a missing field, the upstream pipeline-context call fails silently (all are `.catch((e) => console.error(...))`). User never sees the failure but creators stop getting approved notifications.
findings.fix: Contract suite under `src/app/api/notifications/__tests__/`.

one_week_plan.day_1: Wire vitest + vitest config + jsdom. Add `test` script. Update CI ci.yml with Test step. Migrate aspect-resolver.test.ts to vitest. (TEST-007)
one_week_plan.day_2: Land TEST-001 (load fallback), TEST-002 (workspace_id always), TEST-003 (isValidUuid guard parametric). These three lock the iron law in JS-only unit tests with mocked supabase. (Critical)
one_week_plan.day_3: Land TEST-004 (provision order), TEST-005 (delete rollback), TEST-014 (move rollback), TEST-015 (realtime dedup), TEST-016 (timezone math).
one_week_plan.day_4: Route tests: TEST-008 (provision), TEST-009 (publish-jobs), TEST-010 (team/invite), TEST-013 (require.ts), TEST-011/12 (audit). All run under vitest with mocked Supabase clients.
one_week_plan.day_5: TEST-021 static-grep iron-law guards. TEST-017 commit database.types.ts + db:types:check in CI.
one_week_plan.day_6_7: TEST-006 + TEST-020 — spin up local Supabase via `supabase start` in a CI job, apply all migrations, run trigger integration tests + idempotency tests.

next_steps_after_week_1: Playwright E2E suite covering the 3 board flows (TEST-019)
Coverage gate: start at 0% threshold, ratchet to 30% by month 2, 60% by month 6, blocking PRs below floor
Add a `test` job dependency on the `quality` job so lint/typecheck must pass first
Wire vitest UI for local dev (`npm run test:ui`)
MSW handlers for Supabase + OpenAI to share across all route tests
